node.statement: DIRSUM-PROJ-001: In the finite coordinate direct-sum model for a finite index type iota and dependent coordinate family kappa, the component inclusion maps kappa i -> C into flattened coordinates Sigma kappa -> C and component projection maps Sigma kappa -> C to kappa i -> C satisfy the coordinate biproduct equations: projection after same-component inclusion is the identity, projection after different-component inclusion is zero, and the finite sum over i of inclusion_i after projection_i is the identity on flattened coordinates. This is only the finite coordinate analogue of the sourced direct-sum equations pi_i o iota_j = delta_{i=j} id and sum_j iota_j o pi_j = id; it does not construct categorical biproducts, Q, tensor powers, Hom functors, dagger categories, or direct-sum universal properties.
